exercises.title: Trace AutoCloseable close() call order
exercises.scenario: close() is called when try exits.
exercises.expected_solution:
R1 opened
using R1
R1 closed
after try

test_cases.expected:
R1 opened
using R1
R1 closed
after try

hints: Constructor runs first: 'R1 opened'
Try body: 'using R1'
Try exits: close() called automatically → 'R1 closed'
Execution continues: 'after try'

exercises.title: Multiple resources — closing order
exercises.scenario: Two resources close in reverse declaration order.
exercises.expected_solution:
open: A
open: B
working
close: B
close: A

test_cases.expected:
open: A
open: B
working
close: B
close: A

hints: Open in declaration order: A then B
Close in REVERSE: B then A (LIFO)
Ensures A is still open when B closes (B might depend on A)

exercises.title: Simple try-with-resources
exercises.scenario: Use try-with-resources with a custom AutoCloseable.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `FileSimulator implements AutoCloseable` with name field. open() prints 'opened: name', readLine() returns 'line from name', close() prints 'closed: name'. Use it in try-with-resources.

Expected output:
opened: data.txt
line from data.txt
closed: data.txt
exercises.starter_code: // FileSimulator with AutoCloseable, use in try-with-resources

test_cases.expected_stdout:
opened: data.txt
line from data.txt
closed: data.txt

hints: implements AutoCloseable and override close()
try (FileSimulator fs = new FileSimulator('data.txt')) { ... }
close() prints closed automatically when try exits

exercises.title: close() called even on exception
exercises.scenario: Resource is closed regardless of whether try body throws.
exercises.instructions:
Use try-with-resources. Throw an exception inside the try block and confirm the resource is still closed.

Expected output:
Resource opened
Resource closed
Caught: Simulated error
exercises.starter_code: // Show close() is called even when exception thrown in try body

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Resource opened
Resource closed
Caught: Simulated error

hints: close() is called BEFORE the catch block runs
Output order: opened → closed → caught message
This is the core guarantee of try-with-resources

exercises.title: Read simulated file line by line
exercises.scenario: Classic file reading pattern with try-with-resources.
exercises.instructions:
Use BufferedReader + StringReader (simulates FileReader) to read lines and count them.

Content: 'line1\nline2\nline3'

Expected output:
line1
line2
line3
Total lines: 3
exercises.starter_code:
import java.io.*;

// BufferedReader in try-with-resources to read simulated file content
hints: try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(new StringReader(content)))
readLine() returns null at end of stream
lineCount variable declared outside try — accessible after block

exercises.title: Write to StringWriter with PrintWriter
exercises.instructions:
Use PrintWriter + StringWriter (simulates file writing) to write a report. Read the result after the try block.

Expected output:
Name: Aisha
Score: 95
Grade: A
exercises.starter_code:
import java.io.*;

// PrintWriter in try-with-resources to write simulated report
hints: StringWriter sw declared outside try — accessible after to read result
try (PrintWriter pw = new PrintWriter(sw))
close() flushes — important for buffered writers

exercises.title: Idempotent AutoCloseable
exercises.scenario: close() safe to call multiple times.
exercises.instructions:
Implement `DatabaseConnection implements AutoCloseable` with a `closed` boolean flag. `close()` should only print 'disconnected' ONCE — second call is ignored. Show both uses.

Expected output:
Connected
Disconnected
close() again: no-op
connected: false
exercises.starter_code: // Idempotent AutoCloseable — close() safe to call multiple times

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Connected
Disconnected
close() again: no-op
connected: false

hints: private boolean closed = false
if (!closed) { ... closed = true; } in close()
Second call to close(): closed is true, condition false, nothing printed

exercises.title: Multiple resources — three in one try
exercises.instructions:
Create three AutoCloseable resources: `Connection`, `Statement`, `ResultSet` (simulated). Declare all three in one try-with-resources. Print opening/closing order.

Expected output:
Connection opened
Statement opened
ResultSet opened
Querying...
ResultSet closed
Statement closed
Connection closed
exercises.starter_code: // Three resources in one try — demonstrates LIFO closing order

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Connection opened
Statement opened
ResultSet opened
Querying...
ResultSet closed
Statement closed
Connection closed

hints: Three resources in one try header, separated by semicolons
Opened in order: Conn, Stmt, Rs
Closed in reverse: Rs, Stmt, Conn

exercises.title: Suppressed exceptions
exercises.scenario: Both try body and close() throw — suppressed is attached.
exercises.instructions:
Create a resource where close() always throws. Throw a different exception in the try body. Show that the primary exception propagates and the close() exception is suppressed (accessible via getSuppressed()).

Expected output:
Primary: body failed
Suppressed: close failed
exercises.starter_code: // Resource that throws in both work() and close()

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Primary: body failed
Suppressed: close failed

hints: 'body failed' is the primary exception from work()
'close failed' from close() is suppressed — attached to primary
e.getSuppressed() returns array of suppressed exceptions

exercises.title: Try-with-resources + catch + finally
exercises.instructions:
Combine try-with-resources with catch and finally. Show the order: resource closes before catch and before finally.

Expected output:
Resource opened
Error occurred
Resource closed
Handled: oops
Finally runs

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Resource opened
Error occurred
Resource closed
Handled: oops
Finally runs

hints: Order: try body (partial) → resource close → catch → finally
Resource closed BEFORE catch block runs
Finally still runs after catch

exercises.title: Refactor finally to try-with-resources
hints: Declare BufferedReader in the try header
No null check needed — if constructor throws, nothing to close
The entire 5-line finally block disappears

exercises.title: Report generator with two resources
exercises.scenario: Reading and writing simultaneously with two resources.
exercises.instructions:
Read CSV data (via StringReader → BufferedReader), parse it, write a report (via StringWriter → PrintWriter). Both resources in try-with-resources.

Input CSV: 'Aisha,A\nRaj,B\nPriya,A'

Expected output:
=== Grade Report ===
Aisha: A
Raj: B
Priya: A
A count: 2, B count: 1

test_cases.expected_stdout:
=== Grade Report ===
Aisha: A
Raj: B
Priya: A
A count: 2, B count: 1

hints: Two resources: BufferedReader + PrintWriter in same try header
StringWriter declared OUTSIDE try — to read result after close
Closes in reverse: PrintWriter (declared 2nd) first, then BufferedReader

exercises.title: Full AutoCloseable with state guard
exercises.scenario: Custom resource with full defensive implementation.
exercises.instructions:
Implement `TransactionManager implements AutoCloseable`:
- Opens on construction, sets `active = true`
- `execute(String sql)` throws `IllegalStateException` if not active
- `commit()` marks committed
- `close()` — if committed, prints 'committed and closed'; if not, prints 'rolled back and closed'. Idempotent.

Expected output:
Transaction started
execute: INSERT users
execute: UPDATE accounts
committed and closed
Rolled back (not committed):
Transaction started
execute: DELETE logs
rolled back and closed
exercises.starter_code: // Full TransactionManager with state guard and AutoCloseable

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Transaction started
execute: INSERT users
execute: UPDATE accounts
committed and closed
Rolled back (not committed):
Transaction started
execute: DELETE logs
rolled back and closed

hints: Three state flags: active, committed, closed
close() checks committed flag to decide commit vs rollback
Idempotent: if (closed) return; at the top of close()

exercises.title: Batch file processor — resource pipeline
exercises.scenario: Read multiple simulated files with try-with-resources in a loop.
exercises.instructions:
Process 3 'files' (simulated with StringReader). For each: open a reader in try-with-resources, count words, accumulate total. Handle individual file failures without stopping the batch.

Files: 'hello world java', 'one two', 'this is a test line'

Expected output:
File 1: 3 words
File 2: 2 words
File 3: 5 words
Total words: 10
exercises.starter_code:
import java.io.*;

// Process each file in its own try-with-resources

test_cases.expected_stdout:
File 1: 3 words
File 2: 2 words
File 3: 5 words
Total words: 10

hints: Each file gets its own try-with-resources in the loop
Per-file catch handles failure without stopping other files
split('\\s+') splits on any whitespace — handles multiple spaces

exercises.title: Complete JDBC-style resource chain
exercises.scenario: All Module 7 exception tools in a realistic JDBC simulation.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate a complete JDBC query lifecycle with custom AutoCloseable classes:
- `SimConnection` (opens/closes connection pool slot)
- `SimStatement` (depends on connection)
- `SimResultSet` (depends on statement, iterates rows)

All three in one try-with-resources. Process results. Handle a 'bad SQL' scenario that throws mid-query. Show suppressed exceptions work correctly.

Expected output:
Conn opened
Stmt prepared: SELECT * FROM users
RS opened
Row: Aisha | Engineering
Row: Raj | Sales
RS closed
Stmt closed
Conn closed
---
Conn opened
Stmt prepared: BAD SQL
Primary error: Syntax error in: BAD SQL
RS closed (suppressed in real JDBC)
Stmt closed
Conn closed
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Full JDBC-style AutoCloseable chain simulation
hints: Three resources: Connection, Statement, ResultSet — nested or combined try
BAD SQL: SimResultSet constructor throws after printing 'RS opened'
Connection and Statement close regardless (outer try-with-resources)
Suppressed exceptions from RS.close() attach to the primary exception
